title: Non Executive Director
summary:
I originally trained in Hotel Management where I progressed to GM level and I had the privilege of working in many of Dublin’s best hotels.
In 1989, I switched career to advising on the hotel property market and was the founding Director of CBRE Ireland's specialist Hotels Division. In that role I worked on many notable Hotel deals including the sale of the Shelbourne Hotel (twice), Gresham, Four Seasons and Burlington hotels, Great Southern Hotel Group, several Jury’s Inns, Newpark Kilkenny, Metropole Cork, Dublin's Morrison, Clarion & Trinity Capital, Limerick’s Strand hotel and the Temple Bar Hotel. 
Highlight Resort deals include Lough Erne, Glenlo Abbey, Moyvalley, Lyrath and the Carton House Estate. Overseas deals include the Savoy Group in London and the Four Seasons in Milan (for a record €1m per key).
In December 2017 I left CBRE Hotels to focus on my private clients – identifying opportunities & placing hotels for them, preferably off-market. This is now the principal ongoing activity for my company, DC CURTIN & ASSOCIATES Ltd.

In early 2018, I was invited to join the Board of iNua Hospitality plc as a non executive director. iNua are a very progressive and rapidly expanding Irish Hotel group with eight 4 & 5 star top class regional properties and one other under management contract. Covid 19 has put further expansion of the group on hold for the moment but once the hotel market reopens, iNua will be looking again for suitable acquisition opportunities. I am delighted to bring my experience and knowledge of the Irish hotel market to the benefit of iNua's very hard working executive management team and to their investors.

I am a member of the Institute of Professional Auctioneers & Valuers and also a longstanding member of the Irish Hospitality Institute.

title: John Sheanon Director
summary:
Who?

CFAT is a group of highly dedicated leaders in their respective fields.  From property investment, engineering, through to quantity surveying and planning, CFAT combines the very best experts to ensure a realistic approach to property investment, asset recovery and disposal. 

The Group, established in 2010, is made up of the following members: 
Kerrigan Sheanon Newman, O'Neill Strategic Property Advice, NRB Consulting Engineers (Traffic), Waterman Moylan Engineering Consultants, Tony Bamford Planning and JDA Architects. 

Why?

The property market needs a 'one stop shop' with experts in valuation, planning, traffic, environment, costing and architecture.

What can CFAT help me with?

We can help you to identify what sort of asset you have and identify its liabilities.
We can highlight issues you need to be aware of with an asset and provide recommendations on how to resolve those issues. 
We can identify the market for your asset and the costs of marketing. 
We can provide full due diligence when purchasing an asset or portfolio of assets. 

What makes CFAT different?

A number of consortia sprang up over the past 5 years.  CFAT is different to them all.  Jobs aren't allocated through a single point of contact. We operate as a team, meeting to discuss projects as they mature, sharing specialist knowledge, collaborating to provide you with a single report that combines the inputs of our specialists.

title: Chief Executive Officer
summary: I lead General Paints Group with a simple belief: strong businesses are built by great people, guided by clear values and united behind a shared purpose.Our purpose is clear — to positively impact the world we live in.As CEO, I’m responsible for shaping the long-term direction of the Group across strategy, brand growth, culture and performance. We are the parent company behind Colourtrend, Curator and Cove & Co. — three distinctive brands rooted in craftsmanship, creativity and commercial strength.Our mission is to embrace innovation to create lasting, vibrant colour that protects our planet, so we can build better futures for our people, customers, partners and communities. That commitment is not aspirational — it is embedded in how we operate. We are proud to be Ireland’s first BCorp-accredited paint company, holding ourselves accountable to the highest environmental and social standards.People and culture sit at the centre of our strategy. We are building a high-performance organisation grounded in Respect, Excellence and Partnership — where teams are empowered, collaboration is expected, and leadership is measured by the success of others. Our BCorp values reinforce this commitment: balancing commercial ambition with responsibility, long-term thinking and meaningful impact.Our strategy is anchored in three pillars:• People & Culture – attracting, developing and empowering exceptional talent• Enterprise Excellence – disciplined execution, innovation and operational strength• Sustainability – embedding responsibility into how we manufacture, source and serveWe are focused on scaling our brands across Ireland, the UK and international markets, strengthening customer relationships and investing in the capabilities that will define the next chapter of growth.General Paints Group is building a modern, purpose-led paint business — commercially strong, culturally aligned and committed to lasting impact.
titleDescription:
At General Paints Group, our purpose is simple:
To positively impact the world we live in.

As CEO, I lead a portfolio of brands — Colourtrend, Curator and Cove & Co. — united by a belief that colour shapes how people live, feel and create. Our mission is to embrace innovation to create lasting, vibrant colour that protects our planet, so we can build better futures for our people, customers, partners and communities.

We are proud to be Ireland’s first BCorp-accredited paint company, holding ourselves to the highest environmental and social standards while building a commercially strong, future-focused business.

Our strategy is anchored in three pillars:

People & Culture – empowering talented teams to perform and grow.
Enterprise Excellence – disciplined execution, operational strength and data-led decisions.
Sustainability – embedding responsibility into how we manufacture, source and serve.

I previously served as CFO of the Group, leading enterprise-wide transformation across finance, procurement and IT, work that strengthened our operational backbone and gave us the clarity to scale with confidence.

Today, my focus is broader: shaping long-term strategy, investing in brand growth, expanding distribution across Ireland, the UK and international markets, and building a high-performance leadership team aligned behind shared values — Respect, Excellence and Partnership.

We are building a modern paint group — creatively inspired, commercially disciplined and purpose-led.

Learn more:
www.generalpaintsgroup.com

title: Financial Controller & Transformation Lead
summary:
I am an accomplished Chartered Accountant (FCA) with a career defined by the ability to navigate the granular complexities of financial data while driving the high-level strategic transformations that modern, large-scale enterprises require. Across my tenure in publicly listed (PLC) and private global organisations, I have established a reputation as a trusted finance partner who bridges the gap between technical accounting excellence and commercial growth. 
My experience spans large PLC's, pivate companies and joint ventures across eCommerce Technology, Telecoms, Media, and FMCG. Most recently, as Interim Director of Finance Transformation at ESW (€1BN+ global eCommerce), I have spearheaded the evolution of finance operations, from leading a large-scale ERP implementation, to adopting RPA/Alteryx, to piloting Agentic AI use cases.
I thrive in environments where legacy processes meet modern digital demands. My toolkit combines deep expertise in IFRS, M&A valuation, and FP&A with a forward-thinking advocacy for AI and Digital Transformation. I don’t just report the numbers I build the systems that optimise them.

titleDescription:
TRIVIUMVET - BRIDGING THE TREATMENT GAPS IN COMPANION ANIMAL HEALTH
At TriviumVet we believe that our pets are part of our family and our expectations are that they live longer, healthier and happier lives….when they get sick we want the best healthcare solutions possible. That’s why we have a world class team of clinicians, academics and researchers identifying gaps in available treatments and developing safe and effective pharmaceuticals and diagnostics for companion animals.
